title: senecio crassiflorus var. maritimus
intro: Senecio crassiflorus var. maritimus, also known as the coastal ragwort, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the coastal regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Senecio crassiflorus var. maritimus include its potential to act as an expectorant, helping to clear mucus from the lungs, and its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease pain. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in oil, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as salves and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
